Every data structure, function signature, analysis module must treat methylation type first-class parameter, assumption. package must work equally well 6mA, 4mC (various bacterial MTases), 5mC (Dcm analogs), modification type nanopore callers produce.

title: CLAUDE.md — AI Assistant Guide for comma
text: commaData S4 class — extends SummarizedExperiment; slots: genomeInfo, annotation, motifSites; full validity() show() methods commaData() constructor — dispatches parser caller arg; merges multi-sample matrices using site key (chrom:position:strand:mod_type); applies min_coverage thresholding Modkit parser (.parseModkit) — reads 15-column modkit pileup BED; maps mod codes (→6mA, m→5mC, 21839→4mC); 0-based→1-based conversion Megalodon parser (.parseMegalodon) — per-read aggregation per-site beta values; explicit mod_type required Accessor S4 methods — methylation(), coverage(), sampleInfo(), siteInfo(), modTypes(), genome(), annotation(), motifSites(), [, subset() loadAnnotation() — GFF3/BED → GRanges standardized feature_type/name columns findMotifSites() — BSgenome FASTA + motif regex → GRanges (strands, IUPAC support) Genome utilities — .validateGenomeInfo(), .circularIndex(), .makeSeqinfo() comma_example_data — synthetic commaData: 300 sites (200×6mA, 100×5mC), 3 samples, chr_sim (100 kb), differential ground truth rowData$is_diff Example files — inst/extdata/example_modkit.bed (20 sites: 10×6mA, 5×5mC) inst/extdata/example.gff3 (5 genes chr_sim)

title: CLAUDE.md — AI Assistant Guide for comma
text: annotateSites() — vectorized annotation using GenomicRanges::findOverlaps(); three modes (overlap, proximity, metagene); replaces deleted annotateMethylSites(), annotateTSS(), annotateTTS() slidingWindow() — generalized genome-wide smoothing; stat = “median” | “mean”; genome size always genomeInfo, never hardcoded; uses zoo::rollapply(); replaces deleted methylRollingMedian(), methylRollingMean() methylomeSummary() — per-sample distribution stats (mean/median/SD beta, n_sites, frac_methylated, coverage stats); returns tidy data.frame ggplot2 coverageDepth() — windowed sequencing depth across genome; optional log2 transform; replaces calculateMethylSiteDepth() varianceByDepth() — methylation variance stratified coverage level; replaces varByCoverage() writeBED() — fully rewritten; accepts commaData, output path, sample name; writes BED9 format itemRGB methylation scale; hardcoded paths

title: CLAUDE.md — AI Assistant Guide for comma
text: diffMethyl() — main differential methylation function (modeled DESeq2’s DESeq()); accepts commaData + formula; returns enriched commaData dm_pvalue, dm_padj, dm_delta_beta, dm_mean_beta_<cond> rowData; supports method = "beta_binomial" (default) method = "methylkit" (requires methylKit) results() — S4 method extract diff methylation table tidy data.frame filterResults() — S4 method filter results padj delta_beta thresholds .parseDorado() — full Dorado BAM parser; reads MM/ML tags via Rsamtools::scanBam(), CIGAR-decodes read positions, aggregates per-site beta values; handles 6mA, 5mC, 4mC one BAM beta_binomial.R — internal per-site quasibinomial GLM engine methylkit_wrapper.R — internal methylKit dispatch wrapper multiple_testing.R — internal BH/FDR correction utility

title: Getting Started with comma
text: comma (Comparative Methylomics Microbial Analysis) R package genome-wide analysis bacterial DNA methylation Oxford Nanopore sequencing data. supports three modification types — N6-methyladenine (6mA), 5-methylcytosine (5mC), N4-methylcytosine (4mC) — single, unified data container. vignette walks complete analysis workflow using built-comma_example_data synthetic dataset. typical comma workflow five steps: Load per-sample methylation files commaData object. QC data (coverage, beta distributions, PCA). Annotate sites relative genomic features. Test differential methylation conditions. Visualize results.

title: Getting Started with comma
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("comma") library(comma)

title: Getting Started with comma
text: commaData extends SummarizedExperiment central data container comma. stores: methylation — sites × samples matrix beta values (0–1). coverage — sites × samples matrix read depths. rowData — per-site metadata: chromosome, position, strand, mod_type. colData — per-sample metadata: sample_name, condition, replicate. genomeInfo — chromosome names lengths. annotation — genomic features GRanges object. motifSites — motif instances GRanges object. built-comma_example_data contains 300 synthetic methylation sites (200 × 6mA, 100 × 5mC) simulated 100 kb chromosome across three samples: two controls (ctrl_1, ctrl_2) one treatment (treat_1).
code: data(comma_example_data) comma_example_data #> class: commaData #> sites: 300 | samples: 6  #> mod types: 5mC, 6mA  #> conditions: control, treatment  #> genome: 1 chromosome (100,000 bp total)  #> annotation: 5 features  #> motif sites: none # Modification types present modTypes(comma_example_data) #> [1] "5mC" "6mA"  # Per-sample metadata sampleInfo(comma_example_data) #>         sample_name condition replicate caller #> ctrl_1       ctrl_1   control         1 modkit #> ctrl_2       ctrl_2   control         2 modkit #> ctrl_3       ctrl_3   control         3 modkit #> treat_1     treat_1 treatment         1 modkit #> treat_2     treat_2 treatment         2 modkit #> treat_3     treat_3 treatment         3 modkit  # Matrix dimensions: sites × samples dim(methylation(comma_example_data)) #> [1] 300   6

text: methylomeSummary() returns tidy data frame per-sample distribution statistics:
code: ms <- methylomeSummary(comma_example_data) ms[, c("sample_name", "condition", "mean_beta", "median_beta", "n_covered")] #>   sample_name condition mean_beta median_beta n_covered #> 1      ctrl_1   control 0.8678843   0.8881436       300 #> 2      ctrl_2   control 0.8728354   0.8951648       300 #> 3      ctrl_3   control 0.8781476   0.8966108       300 #> 4     treat_1 treatment 0.8135452   0.8829561       300 #> 5     treat_2 treatment 0.8136529   0.8867238       300 #> 6     treat_3 treatment 0.8004998   0.8694701       300

title: Getting Started with comma
text: plot_coverage() shows distribution sequencing depth per site, per sample. Consistent coverage across samples important quality indicator. Coverage depth distribution per sample.
code: plot_coverage(comma_example_data)

title: Getting Started with comma
text: plot_methylation_distribution() plots density methylation levels sample. Bacterial genomes often show bimodal distribution (sites either fully methylated unmethylated). Methylation beta value density per sample, faceted modification type. Restrict single modification type: Beta value density 6mA sites .
code: plot_methylation_distribution(comma_example_data) plot_methylation_distribution(comma_example_data, mod_type = "6mA")

title: Getting Started with comma
text: plot_pca() performs PCA per-sample methylation profiles. Samples condition cluster together. PCA methylation profiles colored condition.
code: plot_pca(comma_example_data, color_by = "condition")

title: Getting Started with comma
text: annotateSites() maps methylation sites genomic features using three modes: "overlap" — features overlap site. "proximity" — nearest feature site. "metagene" — normalized position within features (TSS = 0, TTS = 1). plot_metagene() visualizes average methylation profile across gene bodies: Mean methylation profile across gene bodies (TSS TTS).
code: annotated <- annotateSites(comma_example_data, type = "overlap") si <- siteInfo(annotated)  # Proportion of sites overlapping at least one annotated feature mean(lengths(si$feature_names) > 0) #> [1] 0.02333333 plot_metagene(comma_example_data, feature = "gene")

title: Getting Started with comma
text: plot_genome_track() produces genome browser–style plot methylation along chromosome region: Genome track first 50 kb chr_sim.
code: plot_genome_track(comma_example_data, chromosome = "chr_sim",                   start = 1L, end = 50000L, mod_type = "6mA")

title: Getting Started with comma
text: diffMethyl() tests site differential methylation conditions. modeled DESeq2’s workflow: pass commaData object design formula, receive back object statistical results rowData. Extract results tidy data frame: Filter significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):
code: cd_dm <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition,                     mod_type = "6mA") cd_dm #> class: commaData #> sites: 300 | samples: 6  #> mod types: 5mC, 6mA  #> conditions: control, treatment  #> genome: 1 chromosome (100,000 bp total)  #> annotation: 5 features  #> motif sites: none res <- results(cd_dm) # Top sites by adjusted p-value head(res[order(res$dm_padj),          c("chrom", "position", "mod_type", "dm_delta_beta", "dm_padj")]) #>                       chrom position mod_type dm_delta_beta     dm_padj #> chr_sim:8907:-:6mA  chr_sim     8907      6mA    -0.6097199 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:52014:+:6mA chr_sim    52014      6mA    -0.7591100 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:69527:+:6mA chr_sim    69527      6mA    -0.6702704 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:72824:-:6mA chr_sim    72824      6mA    -0.1353157 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:62293:-:6mA chr_sim    62293      6mA    -0.6522237 0.012869199 #> chr_sim:9028:-:6mA  chr_sim     9028      6mA    -0.6850134 0.018361742 sig <- filterResults(cd_dm, padj = 0.05, delta_beta = 0.2) cat("Significant sites:", nrow(sig), "\n") #> Significant sites: 7

title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
text: Bacterial genomes frequently carry multiple simultaneous DNA methylation marks: 6mA (N6-methyladenine) — deposited Dam methyltransferase GATC motifs; functions mismatch repair cell-cycle regulation. 5mC (5-methylcytosine) — deposited Dcm methyltransferase CCWGG motifs; roles gene regulation less well characterized bacteria. 4mC (N4-methylcytosine) — deposited restriction-modification systems various sequence motifs; protects foreign DNA. comma stores modification types single commaData object, enabling joint analysis, comparison, visualization across modification types. vignette demonstrates multi-modification workflows using built-comma_example_data dataset, contains 6mA 5mC sites.
code: library(comma) data(comma_example_data)

title: Working with Multiple Modification Types
text: default, diffMethyl() tests modification types present object. Use results(..., mod_type = "6mA") extract type-specific results:
code: dm_all <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition) res_6ma <- results(dm_all, mod_type = "6mA") res_5mc <- results(dm_all, mod_type = "5mC")  cat("6mA: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):",     sum(!is.na(res_6ma$dm_padj) & res_6ma$dm_padj < 0.05 &         abs(res_6ma$dm_delta_beta) >= 0.2, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> 6mA: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2): 6 cat("5mC: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):",     sum(!is.na(res_5mc$dm_padj) & res_5mc$dm_padj < 0.05 &         abs(res_5mc$dm_delta_beta) >= 0.2, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> 5mC: significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2): 1

title: comma: for microbial methylation analysis
text: comma (comma: microbial methylation analysis) R package genome-wide analysis bacterial DNA methylation Oxford Nanopore sequencing data. accepts output modkit, Dorado, Megalodon, supports 6mA, 5mC, 4mC single unified container, covers full analysis workflow raw files quality control, site annotation, differential methylation testing, publication-quality visualization.

title: comma: for microbial methylation analysis
text: commaData S4 class — extends SummarizedExperiment; stores beta values, coverage, sample metadata, genome info, annotations together. Three parsers — modkit pileup BED (primary), Dorado BAM (full MM/ML tag decoding), Megalodon (legacy). Vectorized annotation via GenomicRanges::findOverlaps(); three modes: overlap, proximity, metagene (normalized position within feature bodies). Differential methylation — quasibinomial GLM per site DESeq2-style interface (diffMethyl() → results() → filterResults()). Seven plot_*() functions — coverage QC, beta distributions, PCA, genome tracks, metagene profiles, volcano plots, heatmaps; return ggplot objects customization. bacterial genome — organism-specific values hardcoded anywhere. Multi-modification-type — 6mA, 5mC, 4mC coexist one object; every function accepts mod_type argument work individual types.

text: commaData extends SummarizedExperiment constructed per-sample methylation files: built-synthetic dataset (300 sites, 3 samples, 100 kb genome) included testing demonstrations:
code: library(comma)  # Named vector: sample_name → path to modkit pileup BED files <- c(   ctrl_1  = "path/to/ctrl_rep1.bed",   ctrl_2  = "path/to/ctrl_rep2.bed",   treat_1 = "path/to/treat_rep1.bed",   treat_2 = "path/to/treat_rep2.bed" )  col_data <- data.frame(   sample_name = c("ctrl_1",    "ctrl_2",    "treat_1",    "treat_2"),   condition   = c("control",   "control",   "treatment", "treatment"),   replicate   = c(1L,          2L,          1L,          2L) )  obj <- commaData(   files        = files,   colData      = col_data,   genome       = "path/to/genome.fa",   # FASTA, BSgenome, or named integer vector   annotation   = "path/to/genes.gff3",  # GFF3 or BED (optional)   motif        = "GATC",                # regex motif (optional)   min_coverage = 5L,   caller       = "modkit"               # "modkit", "dorado", or "megalodon" ) library(comma) data(comma_example_data) comma_example_data #> class: commaData #> sites: 300 | samples: 6  #> mod types: 5mC, 6mA  #> conditions: control, treatment  #> genome: 1 chromosome (100,000 bp total)  #> annotation: 5 features  #> motif sites: none

text: diffMethyl() tests site differential methylation conditions. modeled DESeq2’s workflow: pass commaData object design formula, get back object per-site statistics rowData: Extract results tidy data frame filter significant sites: plot_volcano() displays differential methylation landscape — effect size (delta methylation) versus significance (–log10 padj):  plot_heatmap() shows per-sample beta values top differentially methylated sites:
code: cd_dm <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition,                     mod_type = "6mA") res <- results(cd_dm) sig <- filterResults(cd_dm, padj = 0.05, delta_beta = 0.2) cat("Total 6mA sites tested:", nrow(res), "\n") #> Total 6mA sites tested: 300 cat("Significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2):", nrow(sig), "\n") #> Significant sites (padj < 0.05, |Δβ| ≥ 0.2): 7  # Top hits head(res[order(res$dm_padj),          c("chrom", "position", "dm_delta_beta", "dm_padj")]) #>                       chrom position dm_delta_beta     dm_padj #> chr_sim:8907:-:6mA  chr_sim     8907    -0.6097199 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:52014:+:6mA chr_sim    52014    -0.7591100 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:69527:+:6mA chr_sim    69527    -0.6702704 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:72824:-:6mA chr_sim    72824    -0.1353157 0.009737468 #> chr_sim:62293:-:6mA chr_sim    62293    -0.6522237 0.012869199 #> chr_sim:9028:-:6mA  chr_sim     9028    -0.6850134 0.018361742 plot_volcano(res) plot_heatmap(res, cd_dm, n_sites = 30L)

what: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
text: Assigns genomic feature annotations methylation sites stored commaData object using findOverlaps. Three annotation modes available: "overlap" assigns overlapping feature identities site, "proximity" reports features within distance window signed offsets, "metagene" reports fractional positions within every overlapping feature.

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
code: annotateSites(   object,   features = NULL,   type = c("overlap", "proximity", "metagene"),   feature_col = "feature_type",   name_col = "name",   window = 500L )

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
text: object commaData object. features GRanges genomic features annotate . NULL (default), annotation stored object via annotation(object) used. Must mcols columns named feature_col name_col. type Character string specifying annotation mode. One : "overlap" (default) site assigned overlapping feature types names. Sites overlap feature receive length-0 CharacterList elements. "proximity" site assigned features within window bp: names, absolute distances, signed relative positions (negative = upstream; positive = downstream feature TSS). Sites nearby features receive length-0 elements. "metagene" site overlaps feature assigned fractional position within feature (0 = feature start, 1 = feature end) every overlapping feature. Strand-aware: "-" strand features, 0 feature end (highest coordinate) 1 feature start (lowest coordinate). Non-overlapping sites receive length-0 elements. feature_col Character string. Name mcols column features contains feature type (e.g., "feature_type"). Default: "feature_type". name_col Character string. Name mcols column features contains feature name (e.g., "name"). Default: "name". window Integer. Window size base pairs type = "proximity". features within distance returned. Default: 500L.

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
text: commaData object identical object except rowData extended new list-valued annotation columns: type = "overlap": feature_types (CharacterList) feature_names (CharacterList) — overlapping feature types names per site. Intergenic sites: lengths(feature_types) == 0. type = "proximity": nearby_features (CharacterList), distances_to_features (IntegerList), rel_positions (IntegerList) — features within window bp. Sites none: lengths(nearby_features) == 0. type = "metagene": metagene_features (CharacterList) metagene_positions (NumericList) — overlapping feature names fractional positions \([0, 1]\). Non-overlapping sites: lengths(metagene_features) == 0.

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
text: three modes return every matching feature per site. Results stored CharacterList, IntegerList, NumericList columns rowData. Sites overlapping/nearby features receive length-0 list elements; test lengths(col) == 0.

title: Annotate methylation sites relative to genomic features — annotateSites
code: data(comma_example_data) # Overlap annotation using built-in annotation annotated <- annotateSites(comma_example_data) si <- siteInfo(annotated) # All overlapping feature types for the first site: si$feature_types[[1]] #> [1] "gene" # Number of sites that overlap at least one feature: sum(lengths(si$feature_types) > 0) #> [1] 7 # Intergenic sites: sum(lengths(si$feature_types) == 0) #> [1] 293  # Metagene annotation mg <- annotateSites(comma_example_data, type = "metagene") si_mg <- siteInfo(mg) # Metagene positions for the first overlapping site: si_mg$metagene_positions[[which(lengths(si_mg$metagene_positions) > 0)[1]]] #> [1] 0.8877756

text: comma package provides complete toolkit genome-wide analysis bacterial DNA methylation Oxford Nanopore sequencing data. supports three major modification types (6mA, 5mC, 4mC), handles input modkit pileup (primary), Dorado BAM, Megalodon (legacy) callers, provides unified data container, annotation utilities, differential methylation testing, full set visualization functions.

text: files Named character vector mapping sample names file paths. Names must match colData$sample_name. Example: c(ctrl_1 = "/path//ctrl_1.bed", treat_1 = "/path//treat_1.bed"). colData data.frame one row per sample. Must contain columns sample_name, condition, replicate. Additional columns (e.g., file_path, batch) preserved. genome Genome size information: named integer vector chromosome sizes (e.g., c(chr1 = 4641652L)), path FASTA file, BSgenome object. Set NULL omit genome information (recommended). annotation Optional. Path GFF3 BED annotation file, pre-loaded GRanges object. NULL, annotation slot left empty. mod_type Optional character vector specifying modification types retain (e.g., "6mA" c("6mA", "5mC")). NULL, modification types detected files kept. motif Optional character string. DNA sequence motif (e.g., "GATC") locate genome. Requires genome FASTA path BSgenome object (named integer vector). NULL, motifSites slot left empty. min_coverage Integer. Minimum read depth include site. Sites present sample coverage threshold beta value set NA. Sites absent sample entirely also NA. Default 5. caller Character string specifying methylation caller produced input files. One "modkit" (default), "megalodon", "dorado".

what: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
text: main function differential methylation analysis comma. Analogous DESeq2::DESeq(), diffMethyl() accepts commaData object, fits statistical model methylation site, returns object enriched per-site test results stored new columns rowData.

title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
code: diffMethyl(   object,   formula = ~condition,   method = c("beta_binomial", "methylkit"),   mod_type = NULL,   min_coverage = 5L,   p_adjust_method = "BH",   ... )

title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
text: object commaData object least two samples distinct conditions. formula one-sided formula specifying design. RHS variable must match column sampleInfo(object) (e.g., ~ condition). Default ~ condition. method Character string selecting statistical backend. "beta_binomial" (default) uses quasibinomial GLM via base R. "methylkit" wraps methylKit::calculateDiffMeth(), requiring methylKit installed. mod_type Character vector NULL. Modification type(s) test (e.g., "6mA", c("6mA", "5mC")). NULL (default), modification types present object tested. min_coverage Integer. Minimum per-sample read depth required include site testing. Sites sample coverage threshold treated NA sample. Default 5L. p_adjust_method Character string. Multiple testing correction method, passed p.adjust. Default "BH" (Benjamini-Hochberg). options: "bonferroni", "holm", "", "none". ... Additional arguments (reserved future use).

title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
text: input commaData object additional columns rowData: dm_pvalue, dm_padj, dm_delta_beta, one dm_mean_beta_<condition> column per condition level. metadata slot updated analysis parameters result column names.

title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
text: Statistical model (method = "beta_binomial"): per-site quasibinomial GLM fitted using glm: $$   \text{Binomial}(n_{\text{mod}},\ n_{\text{total}}) \sim \text{condition} $$ \(n_{\text{mod}} = \text{round}(\beta \times \text{coverage})\) \(n_{\text{total}} = \text{coverage}\). quasibinomial family accounts overdispersion. P-values extracted Wald t-test contrast coefficient. method requires additional packages beyond base R. Alternative model (method = "methylkit"): Wraps methylKit::calculateDiffMeth(), uses logistic regression. Requires methylKit installed (BiocManager::install("methylKit")). Returns results standardised format. Multiple mod types: mod_type = NULL (default), modification types present object tested independently results combined. Sites mod type tested receive NA dm_* columns. Small-sample note: Differential methylation testing replicates (e.g., n = 1 per group) mathematically possible extremely low statistical power. Treat results exploratory . Result columns added rowData: dm_pvalue Raw p-value GLM Wald test. dm_padj Adjusted p-value (Benjamini-Hochberg default). dm_delta_beta Effect size: mean methylation treatment group minus mean methylation reference (control) group. Positive values indicate hypermethylation treatment. dm_mean_beta_<condition> One column per condition level (named actual condition values), containing per-group mean beta value site. Analysis parameters result column names stored metadata(object)$diffMethyl_params metadata(object)$diffMethyl_result_cols.

title: Identify differentially methylated sites between conditions — diffMethyl
code: data(comma_example_data) # Test for differential 6mA methylation between conditions dm <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, formula = ~ condition, mod_type = "6mA")  # How many sites are significant? rd <- as.data.frame(SummarizedExperiment::rowData(dm)) sum(rd$dm_padj < 0.05 & abs(rd$dm_delta_beta) >= 0.2, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 7

what: Per-site quasibinomial GLM for differential methylation — .betaBinomialTest

title: Per-site quasibinomial GLM for differential methylation — .betaBinomialTest
code: .betaBinomialTest(methyl_mat, coverage_mat, coldata, formula)

title: Per-site quasibinomial GLM for differential methylation — .betaBinomialTest
text: methyl_mat Numeric matrix (sites × samples) beta values. NA indicates -coverage sites. coverage_mat Integer matrix (sites × samples) read depths. coldata data.frame least one column matching RHS variable formula (typically condition). formula One-sided formula specifying design (e.g., ~ condition).

title: Per-site quasibinomial GLM for differential methylation — .betaBinomialTest
text: data.frame one row per site (row order methyl_mat), containing: pvalue Raw p-value Wald test condition coefficient. NA untestable sites. delta_beta Estimated effect size: mean beta treatment group minus mean beta reference (control) group. Uses model matrix reference level baseline. NA untestable sites. mean_beta_<level> One column per unique condition level, named mean_beta_<level> (e.g., mean_beta_control, mean_beta_treatment). Contains per-group observed mean beta value computed directly non-NA data.

title: Per-site quasibinomial GLM for differential methylation — .betaBinomialTest
text: model treats methylation counts overdispersed binomial data: site, number modified reads modelled \(\text{Binomial}(n_{\text{coverage}}, p)\) quasibinomial family account overdispersion. formula fitted site--site using glm. Small-sample caveat: replicates (e.g., 2 control + 1 treatment), residual degrees freedom small (df = 1 case). quasibinomial dispersion estimate resulting p-values remain mathematically valid low power. Interpret results n < 3 per group caution. Sites skipped (result NA) : samples NA beta values (low coverage). Fewer 2 non-NA samples present. GLM fails converge contrast coefficient extracted (e.g., singular fit, one condition level present NA removal).

title: Parse a Dorado BAM file with MM/ML base modification tags — .parseDorado
text: function reads BAM using scanBam processes MM/ML tags aligned read. MM tag encodes bases carry modifications positions within read sequence (inter-base offsets); parallel ML tag provides modification probabilities (0–255 scaled 0–1). base called modified ML probability \(> 0.5\). Per-site statistics aggregated counting modified reads (\(n_{\text{mod}}\)) total reads genomic position (\(n_{\text{total}}\)). beta value \(\beta = n_{\text{mod}} / n_{\text{total}}\). CIGAR operations used map read positions reference coordinates. Soft-clipped (S) hard-clipped (H) bases excluded; insertions () consume read positions without advancing reference. Recommended workflow: users, simpler first run modkit pileup Dorado BAM, load resulting BED file caller = "modkit". Direct BAM parsing provided users prefer avoid modkit step.

text: Produces volcano plot differential methylation results computed diffMethyl() extracted results(). Sites colored significance category based user-supplied adjusted p-value delta-beta thresholds.

title: Volcano plot for differential methylation results — plot_volcano
code: plot_volcano(results, delta_beta_threshold = 0.2, padj_threshold = 0.05)

title: Volcano plot for differential methylation results — plot_volcano
text: results data.frame returned results(), containing minimum columns dm_delta_beta (numeric, effect size beta difference treatment minus control) dm_padj (numeric, BH-adjusted p-value \([0, 1]\)). delta_beta_threshold Numeric scalar (0, 1). Sites |dm_delta_beta| >= delta_beta_threshold dm_padj <= padj_threshold considered significant. Default 0.2. padj_threshold Numeric scalar (0, 1). Adjusted p-value cutoff significance. Default 0.05.

title: Volcano plot for differential methylation results — plot_volcano
text: ggplot object. x-axis shows dm_delta_beta (effect size), y-axis shows -log10(dm_padj) (significance). Sites colored "Hypermethylated" (positive delta-beta, significant), "Hypomethylated" (negative delta-beta, significant), "significant". Dashed lines mark threshold boundaries. Sites NA adjusted p-value excluded plot.

title: Volcano plot for differential methylation results — plot_volcano
code: data(comma_example_data) cd_dm <- diffMethyl(comma_example_data, ~ condition) res <- results(cd_dm) plot_volcano(res)   # Custom thresholds plot_volcano(res, delta_beta_threshold = 0.3, padj_threshold = 0.01)

title: Extract differential methylation results as a tidy data frame — results
text: data.frame one row per methylation site, containing: chrom Chromosome name. position 1-based genomic position. strand Strand ("+" "-"). mod_type Modification type (e.g., "6mA"). dm_pvalue Raw p-value statistical test. dm_padj Adjusted p-value (Benjamini-Hochberg default). dm_delta_beta Effect size: mean methylation treatment group minus mean methylation reference group. dm_mean_beta_<condition> One column per condition level per-group mean beta values. annotation columns present rowData(object) (e.g., annotateSites) also included.

what: Sliding window methylation summary along the genome — slidingWindow
title: Sliding window methylation summary along the genome — slidingWindow
text: Computes per-position sliding window statistic (median mean) methylation beta values sample commaData object. genome size chromosome read genome(object), organism-specific values ever hardcoded. Circular genome wrap-around supported via .circularIndex().

title: Sliding window methylation summary along the genome — slidingWindow
code: slidingWindow(   object,   window,   stat = c("median", "mean"),   mod_type = NULL,   circular = TRUE )

title: Sliding window methylation summary along the genome — slidingWindow
text: object commaData object. Must genome size information genome(object) (.e., must constructed genome argument). window Positive integer. Window size base pairs. smoothed value position \(p\) computed positions \([p - \lfloor w/2 \rfloor,\; p + \lfloor w/2 \rfloor]\). stat Character string. Summary statistic apply within window. One "median" (default) "mean". mod_type Character string NULL. provided, sites specified modification type (e.g., "6mA") included smoothing. NULL (default), sites used. circular Logical. TRUE (default), positions ends chromosome wrapped around window position 1 can draw positions near chromosome end, vice versa. Appropriate circular bacterial chromosomes.

title: Sliding window methylation summary along the genome — slidingWindow
text: data.frame one row per (chromosome, position, sample) combination, containing: chrom Chromosome name (character). position Genomic position, 1-based (integer). sample_name Sample identifier (character). window_stat Smoothed beta value (numeric). column named window_median window_mean depending stat.

title: Sliding window methylation summary along the genome — slidingWindow
text: positions genome methylation site, beta value vector chromosome-sample pair sparse (mostly NA). zoo::rollapply called na.rm = TRUE windows spanning regions data still produce value least one site present. Positions every site window NA (.e., coverage) remain NA output.

title: Sliding window methylation summary along the genome — slidingWindow
code: data(comma_example_data) # \donttest{ sw <- slidingWindow(comma_example_data, window = 5000L) head(sw) #>     chrom position sample_name window_median #> 1 chr_sim        1      ctrl_1     0.8581977 #> 2 chr_sim        2      ctrl_1     0.8581977 #> 3 chr_sim        3      ctrl_1     0.8581977 #> 4 chr_sim        4      ctrl_1     0.8581977 #> 5 chr_sim        5      ctrl_1     0.8581977 #> 6 chr_sim        6      ctrl_1     0.8581977 # Filter to one sample sw_ctrl1 <- sw[sw$sample_name == "ctrl_1", ] # }

what: Export methylation data as a BED file — writeBED
title: Export methylation data as a BED file — writeBED
text: Writes per-site methylation beta values single sample commaData object 9-column BED file (BED9 format), suitable visualisation IGV, UCSC Genome Browser, genome browsers support itemRGB field.

title: Export methylation data as a BED file — writeBED
code: writeBED(   object,   file,   sample,   mod_type = NULL,   rgb_scale = TRUE,   track_name = sample,   track_description = "methylation beta values" )

title: Export methylation data as a BED file — writeBED
text: object commaData object. file Character string. Path output BED file. file created overwritten. sample Character string. Name sample export. Must match column name methylation(object). mod_type Character string NULL. provided, sites specified modification type written (e.g., "6mA"). NULL (default), sites written. rgb_scale Logical. TRUE (default), itemRGB column added based methylation score using blue--red gradient (low = blue, high = red). FALSE, itemRGB set "0,0,0" sites. track_name Character string. Name shown genome browser track header. Defaults sample. track_description Character string. Description shown track header. Defaults "methylation beta values".

title: Export methylation data as a BED file — writeBED
text: Invisibly returns path written file (file).

title: Export methylation data as a BED file — writeBED
text: BED score field (column 5) contains beta value multiplied 1000 rounded nearest integer (range 0–1000), standard convention methylation BED files. itemRGB colour gradient transitions follows: score ≤ 200 = blue (0,0,255), score ≤ 400 = blue-purple (83,0,172), score ≤ 600 = purple (167,0,85), score ≤ 800 = red-purple (222,0,28), score ≤ 1000 = red (250,0,0). Sites NA methylation (coverage threshold) excluded output.

title: Export methylation data as a BED file — writeBED
code: data(comma_example_data) tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".bed") writeBED(comma_example_data, file = tmp, sample = "ctrl_1", mod_type = "6mA")  # \donttest{ # Write to a permanent file writeBED(comma_example_data,          file    = "ctrl_1_methylation.bed",          sample  = "ctrl_1",          mod_type = "6mA") # }

title: comma 0.6.0
text: Version bumped 0.6.0 Bioconductor submission preparation. Package passes R CMD check zero code-level errors warnings. Remaining check notes environment-specific (locale, pdflatex availability) present Bioconductor’s check infrastructure. Full roxygen2 documentation exported functions, including \donttest{} examples throughout.

title: comma 0.5.0
text: plot_methylation_distribution() — beta value density plot per sample, coloured sample name, faceted modification type multiple types present. Useful QC comparing methylation level distributions. plot_genome_track() — genome browser-style scatter plot methylation beta values vs. genomic position. Supports positional windowing via start/ end arguments, mod_type filtering, optional feature annotation rectangles annotation(object). plot_metagene() — average methylation profile across class genomic features, normalized fractional position [0 = TSS, 1 = TTS]. Uses annotateSites(type = "metagene") internally. plot_volcano() — volcano plot differential methylation results. Accepts data.frame output results(). Colors points Hypermethylated, Hypomethylated, significant based user-supplied padj_threshold delta_beta_threshold. plot_heatmap() — geom_tile heatmap top differentially methylated sites (ranked adjusted p-value) across samples. Uses ggplot2 ; ComplexHeatmap dependency required. plot_pca() — PCA per-sample methylation profiles using stats::prcomp(). Points colored optionally shaped column sampleInfo(object). plot_coverage() — histogram sequencing depth per site, per sample. Useful coverage QC differential methylation testing.

title: comma 0.5.0
text: "Getting Started comma" — end--end workflow using comma_example_data. "Working Multiple Modification Types" — joint 6mA + 5mC analysis. Added package-level documentation (?comma) describing overall workflow.

title: comma 0.5.0
text: Added BiocStyle, ComplexHeatmap, ggrepel, patchwork Suggests. None required core functions; patchwork enables combined annotation tracks plot_genome_track() plot_heatmap(). Added VignetteBuilder: knitr DESCRIPTION. Added Visualization biocViews.

title: comma 0.4.0
text: diffMethyl() — core differential methylation function. Accepts commaData object design formula, tests methylation site differential methylation conditions, returns object enriched per-site statistics rowData. Supports two statistical backends: method = "beta_binomial" (default): per-site quasibinomial GLM via base R stats::glm(). extra packages required. method = "methylkit": wraps methylKit::calculateDiffMeth() users prefer methylKit pipeline. Requires methylKit installed. New rowData columns: dm_pvalue, dm_padj (Benjamini-Hochberg default), dm_delta_beta (effect size: treatment minus control mean beta), one dm_mean_beta_<condition> column per condition level. Analysis parameters stored metadata(object)$diffMethyl_params. results() — S4 method extract differential methylation results commaData object tidy data.frame. Supports optional mod_type filtering. filterResults() — S4 method filter results table adjusted p-value (padj) absolute effect size (delta_beta) thresholds. .parseDorado() — full Dorado BAM parser replacing previous stub. Reads MM/ML base modification tags Dorado-aligned BAM files using Rsamtools::scanBam(), parses modification positions CIGAR-decoded read coordinates, aggregates per-read calls per-site beta values. Supports 6mA, 5mC, 4mC BAM file. Invoked automatically commaData(..., caller = "dorado").

title: comma 0.4.0
text: Added methylKit Suggests. Required diffMethyl(..., method = "methylkit").

title: comma 0.3.0
text: annotateSites() — new vectorized annotation function replaces annotateMethylSites(), annotateTSS(), annotateTTS(). Accepts commaData object uses GenomicRanges::findOverlaps() internally — nested -loops. Supports three annotation modes: type = "overlap": assigns feature type name site; non-overlapping sites labelled "intergenic". type = "proximity": reports nearest feature, distance (bp), signed relative position relative feature’s TSS. type = "metagene": reports fractional position (0–1) within overlapping feature, strand-aware. Returns updated commaData new columns rowData. slidingWindow() — new generalised sliding window function replaces methylRollingMedian() methylRollingMean(). Accepts commaData object; genome size always read genome(object) — hardcoded values. Supports stat = "median" stat = "mean", per-sample output, mod_type filtering, circular genome wrap-around. Returns tidy data.frame. methylomeSummary() — new function computes per-sample summary statistics (mean/median/SD beta values, fraction methylated, mean/median coverage). Returns tidy data.frame suitable ggplot2 tabular reporting. Supports mod_type filtering. coverageDepth() — new function bins genome non-overlapping windows computes mean median sequencing depth per window per sample. Replaces calculateMethylSiteDepth(). Returns tidy data.frame. varianceByDepth() — new function computes per-sample methylation variance function sequencing depth. Replaces varByCoverage(). Accepts commaData object; hardcoded column names. Returns tidy data.frame. writeBED() — rewritten scratch. Accepts commaData object, output path, sample name. Removes hardcoded developer paths chromosome names. Writes standard BED9 file optional blue--red itemRGB methylation scale.

title: comma 0.3.0
text: annotateMethylSites(), annotateTSS(), annotateTTS() removed. Use annotateSites() instead. methylRollingMedian() methylRollingMean() removed. Use slidingWindow() instead. calculateMethylSiteDepth() varByCoverage() removed (exported). Use coverageDepth() varianceByDepth() instead.

Version bumped 0.3.0.

title: comma 0.3.0
text: Added GenomeInfoDb Imports (transitively available now explicitly declared).

title: comma 0.2.0
text: Added commaData S4 class (extends SummarizedExperiment) — central data object comma analyses. Stores methylation beta values coverage assay matrices per-site per-sample metadata. Added commaData() constructor: parses one modkit pileup BED files (Megalodon files) merges commaData object consistent site × sample matrices. Supports multi-sample, multi-modification-type data. Added modkit parser (.parseModkit()): reads modkit pileup BED output (primary ONT methylation calling format). Supports three modification types: 6mA (), 5mC (m), 4mC (21839). Added Megalodon parser (.parseMegalodon()): reads legacy Megalodon per-read BED output backward compatibility existing datasets. Added Dorado BAM parser stub (.parseDorado()): yet implemented; provides clear error message directing users use modkit pileup first. Added findMotifSites(): locates instances DNA sequence motif (e.g., GATC, CCWGG) genome (BSgenome object FASTA file) returns GRanges positions strands. Added loadAnnotation(): reads GFF3 BED annotation files returns GRanges standardized feature_type name metadata columns. Added accessor functions: methylation(), coverage(), sampleInfo(), siteInfo(), modTypes(), genome(), annotation(), motifSites(). Added subsetting: [ sites/samples subset() filtering mod_type, condition, chrom. Added internal genome utilities: .validateGenomeInfo(), .circularIndex(), .makeSeqinfo().

title: comma 0.2.0
text: Removed 9 MG1655-specific bundled datasets (data/*.rda). specific E. coli K-12 MG1655 usable organisms. Added comma_example_data: synthetic commaData object simulated 100 kb genome, 3 samples (2 conditions), two modification types (6mA 5mC). Used vignettes tests. Run data-raw/create_example_data.R regenerate. Added inst/extdata/example_modkit.bed: small modkit pileup BED constructor tests examples. Added inst/extdata/example.gff3: matching GFF3 annotation example genome.

title: comma 0.2.0

R (>= 4.1.0) now minimum R version requirement. Added biocViews field preparation Bioconductor submission.
